Discovery: Iran war has put state-sponsored cyberattacks on full display, with Chinese (Volt Typhoon, APT41), Russian, and Iranian actors escalating infrastructure attacks in 2026; CNBC spotlighted CrowdStrike as 'the kind of stock to buy during the Iran war' and Wedbush called out a 'buy the dip' opportunity in cybersecurity amid AI-driven autonomous exploit threats.
value_picks.analyst_take: $500M buyback expansion + CFO cited valuation disconnect; 97% customer retention; $5B ARR record; Iran cyberwar demand

catalysts_general: AI data center power management leader; near 52-week highs; only 6% consensus upside; best entry on 8-10% pullback (Tier 3: analyst platforms)

Discovery: Completed $9.5B Boyd Thermal acquisition (March 12, 2026) creating the only 'grid-to-chip' power + liquid cooling platform for AI data centers, with BNP Paribas reiterating top-pick status and RBC raising target to $432 on March 24, 2026; AI rack densities now hitting 300-500kW make liquid cooling mandatory.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Best-in-class AI power management but only 6% upside near highs; ideal on 8-10% pullback to $295-310

catalysts_general: $106.3B FCF; India supply chain migration as tariff hedge; 13% analyst upside; better entry below $255 as tariff uncertainty persists (Tier 3: analyst platforms)
growth_picks.analyst_take: $106.3B FCF exceptional; India supply chain migration reduces tariff risk; 13% analyst upside; Trump tariff pressure creates better entry

catalysts_general: XPO ecosystem 100-partner milestone April 2026; new transceivers 75% rack footprint reduction; $2.8M institutional call buying; only 7% upside at current price (Tier 4: institutional flow data)

Discovery: XPO ecosystem hit 100-partner milestone in April 2026 just weeks after launch, with new transceivers cutting rack footprint 75% and delivering 4X bandwidth per rack — directly addresses AI cluster scaling bottleneck for hyperscaler customers.
growth_picks.analyst_take: World-class quality (64% margins, zero debt) but only 6.4% from ATH; analyst consensus $166 = 7% from current; add on pullback

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Nasdaq 100 removal effective April 20 forces index fund liquidation; 84.1% gross margin and $1.5B FCF exceptional; speculative buy only below $60 after forced selling (Tier 3: analyst platforms)

Discovery: AVOID — shares down 60%+ in 2026 as AI coding tools (GitHub Copilot, Cursor) dramatically reduce developer headcount — Atlassian's core customer base (software dev teams) is itself being disrupted, shrinking per-seat demand; on Jefferies AI Risk Basket with continued downside risk.
growth_picks.analyst_take: 84.1% gross margin, $1.5B FCF, 112% analyst upside — but Nasdaq 100 removal April 20 creates forced selling event

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Q1 TCE $107,100/day exceptional; 62% YTD run means poor risk/reward at current highs; ceasefire may reduce tanker premium (Tier 3: analyst platforms)

Discovery: Hormuz closure forcing tanker rerouting around Africa (adding weeks and thousands of miles per voyage) drove VLCC daily charter rates to record $423,736/day; Frontline Q1 2026 TCEs at $107,100/day vs $74,200 in Q4 2025, with 92% coverage — though stock is already up 62% YTD, a fragile ceasefire introduces rerating risk.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Up 62% YTD; Q1 TCEs $107,100/day exceptional but at 52-week highs; hold existing; wait for 15-20% pullback for new entries

catalysts_general: April 22 earnings could revise analyst targets upward; zero consensus upside at current price limits entry discipline; AI infrastructure cooling risk (Tier 3: analyst platforms)

Discovery: Q4 organic orders surged 252% YoY with a $15B backlog (+109% YoY) and book-to-bill of ~2.9x; Citi raised PT to $340 on April 13, 2026 as AI liquid cooling demand accelerates — 2026 revenue guidance $13.25-13.75B at 27-29% organic growth.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Zero consensus upside at current price; April 22 earnings could re-rate targets higher; wait for post-earnings setup

catalysts_general: Book-to-bill ratio >4x exceptional demand signal; analyst target below current price limits entry; AI photonics tailwind real but priced in (Tier 3: analyst platforms)

Discovery: ~50% of revenue from datacom transceiver chips; expanding 6-inch indium phosphide fab to meet XPO 'pluggable-first' demand from AI clusters; benefits from liquid-cooled AI racks enabling high-power coherent lasers — less mature story than Ciena but significant upside if optical supercycle accelerates.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Book-to-bill >4x exceptional signal; but analyst target below current price; valuation stretched relative to fundamentals

catalysts_general: First US positive comps in 8 quarters signals real turnaround progress; +15% YTD run prices most good news; April 28 earnings binary; new CEO Niccol execution risk (Tier 4: Bloomberg, CNBC)

Discovery: Under ex-Chipotle CEO Brian Niccol, turnaround gaining traction: Jefferies upgraded from Underperform to Hold (April 13) on China franchise exit and US stabilization; Tigress Financial reinstated Buy with $122 PT (April 15); +15% YTD with multiple analysts seeing path to $101-122 from current ~$98.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Turnaround real (first US positive comps in 8 quarters) but mostly priced at +15% YTD; April 28 earnings binary; buy below $90

catalysts_general: Royalty model captures value from every chip sold; 97.5% gross margins; but 74x fwd PE at only 7% analyst upside means very narrow margin of safety; beta 3.34 (Tier 3: analyst platforms)
growth_picks.analyst_take: Royalty model captures every AI chip; 97.5% gross margins; but 74x fwd PE with only 7% analyst upside and beta 3.34

catalysts_general: Jefferies flagged EPAM as highly exposed to AI coding tool substitution; revenue growth decelerating ~5%; 9.2x fwd PE but no catalyst to close 50% analyst upside gap (Tier 3: Jefferies research)
growth_picks.analyst_take: IT outsourcing highly exposed to AI coding tools per Jefferies; 9.2x fwd PE but no near-term catalyst; watchlist only

catalysts_general: Terafab SpaceX/Tesla chip collaboration confirmed; Susquehanna PT $65 April 14; 43% run in 30 days from $41.19 low creates poor risk/reward until reset (Tier 2: TechCrunch, Tier 3: Susquehanna)

Discovery: Massive institutional options sweeps on April 9-13: put-selling volume 85x normal at the $64 strike (April 17 expiry, 2.875% yield) and call-buying volume 33x normal at $80 strike (May 8 expiry) — net $11.1M bullish premium; catalyst is the announced 'Terafab' chip-manufacturing collaboration between Intel, SpaceX, and Tesla; stock surged 43% in 30 days from a March 30 low of $41.19.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Terafab real catalyst but stock already +43% from March low; wait for post-Q1 (April 23) pullback before entry

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Tim Cook + CEO Hill cluster insider buy $2.23M; tariff headwind upgraded from $1B to $1.5B; China revenue -20%; FY2027 recovery timeline (Tier 1: Form 4, Tier 3: analyst updates)

Discovery: Three insiders bought in a 5-day cluster at decade-low prices: Director Tim Cook (Apple CEO) spent $1.06M on 25,000 shares (Apr 10), CEO Elliott Hill spent ~$1M on 23,660 shares (Apr 13), and Director John Rogers spent $173K on 4,000 shares (Apr 9) — $2.23M+ total coordinated open-market buying with stock 60%+ below 2021 peak and 30% down YTD at ~$42-44.
growth_picks.analyst_take: $2.23M insider cluster buy sets floor near $42; but $1.5B tariff headwind upgraded; China -20%; recovery timeline FY2027

growth_picks.analyst_take: First-ever GAAP profit achieved; but price targets being cut and stock falling despite profitability; wait for stabilization

catalysts_general: $10B Army contract real; live combat AI genuinely differentiated; Michael Burry short via 2027 puts; 225x PE with beta 2.5+ (Tier 1: SEC 13F Burry position, Tier 3: analyst estimates)

Discovery: Palantir's Project Maven AI is actively running Iran war targeting operations (11,000 US strikes on Iran), powering a $1.3B Pentagon AI contract that is now in live combat use; additionally won $10B Army software contract in Aug 2025 — stock up 40% Q1 2026 but contract pipeline still expanding with new AI warfare applications.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Live combat AI with $10B Army contract — real revenue; but 225x PE and Michael Burry actively short via 2027 puts
